sections.label: Section 583
sections.original:
δίκαια τοὺς τεκόντας ὠφελεῖν τέκνα ,
πατέρα τε πρέσβυν τήν τε κοινωνὸν γάμων .
sections.translation: It is only right that parents should help their children , their aged fathers , and the partners of their marriage .

sections.label: Section 585
sections.original:
πρὸς σοῦ μέν , ὦ παῖ , τοῖς φίλοις τʼ εἶναι φίλον
τά τʼ ἐχθρὰ μισεῖν · ἀλλὰ μὴ ʼπείγου λίαν .
sections.translation: My son , it is like you to show your love for your dear ones and your hate for your enemies ; only curb excessive hastiness .

sections.label: Section 587
sections.original: τί δʼ ἐστὶ τῶνδε θᾶσσον ἢ χρεών , πάτερ ;
sections.translation: How , father , am I now showing more than fitting haste ?

sections.label: Section 588
sections.original:
πολλοὺς πένητας , ὀλβίους δὲ τῷ λόγῳ
δοκοῦντας εἶναι συμμάχους ἄναξ ἔχει ,
sections.translation: The king has a host of allies , needy villains though pretending to be rich ,

sections.label: Section 590
sections.original:
οἳ στάσιν ἔθηκαν καὶ διώλεσαν πόλιν
ἐφʼ ἁρπαγαῖσι τῶν πέλας , τὰ δʼ ἐν δόμοις
δαπάναισι φροῦδα διαφυγόνθʼ ὑπʼ ἀργίας .
ὤφθης ἐσελθὼν πόλιν · ἐπεὶ δʼ ὤφθης , ὅρα
ἐχθροὺς ἀθροίσας μὴ παρὰ γνώμην πέσῃς .
μέλει μὲν οὐδὲν εἴ με πᾶσʼ εἶδεν πόλις ·
sections.translation: who sowed dissension and overthrew the state with a view to plundering their neighbors ; for the wealth they had in their houses was all spent , dissipated by their sloth . You were seen entering the city ; and , that being so , beware that you do not bring your enemies together and be slain unawares .

sections.label: Section 595
sections.original:
ὄρνιν δʼ ἰδών τινʼ οὐκ ἐν αἰσίοις ἕδραις ,
ἔγνων πόνον τινʼ ἐς δόμους πεπτωκότα ·
ὥστʼ ἐκ προνοίας κρύφιος εἰσῆλθον χθόνα .
καλῶς · παρελθὼν νῦν πρόσειπέ θʼ ἑστίαν
sections.translation: Little I care if the whole city saw me ; but happening to see a bird perched in an unlucky position , from it I learned that some trouble had befallen my house ; so on purpose I made my entry to the land by stealth .

sections.label: Section 599
sections.original: καὶ δὸς πατρῴοις δώμασιν σὸν ὄμμʼ ἰδεῖν .
sections.translation: Well done ; now , on your arrival , go salute your household altar ,

sections.label: Section 600
sections.original:
ἥξει γὰρ αὐτὸς σὴν δάμαρτα καὶ τέκνα
ἕλξων φονεύσων κἄμʼ ἐπισφάξων ἄναξ ·
μένοντι δʼ αὐτοῦ πάντα σοι γενήσεται
τῇ τʼ ἀσφαλείᾳ κερδανεῖς · πόλιν δὲ σὴν
μὴ πρὶν ταράξῃς πρὶν τόδʼ εὖ θέσθαι , τέκνον .
sections.translation: and let your father ’ s halls behold your face . For soon the king will be here in person to drag away your wife and children and murder them , and to add me to the bloody list . But if you remain on the spot all will go well , and you will profit by this security ; but do not rouse

sections.label: Section 605
sections.original: δράσω τάδʼ · εὖ γὰρ εἶπας · εἶμʼ ἔσω δόμων .
sections.translation: your city before you have these matters well in train , my son .

sections.label: Section 606
sections.original:
χρόνῳ δʼ ἀνελθὼν ἐξ ἀνηλίων μυχῶν
Ἅιδου Κόρης τʼ ἔνερθεν , οὐκ ἀτιμάσω
θεοὺς προσειπεῖν πρῶτα τοὺς κατὰ στέγας .
ἦλθες γὰρ ὄντως δώματʼ εἰς Ἅιδου , τέκνον ;
sections.translation: I will do so ; your advice is good ; I will enter my house . After my return at length from the sunless den of Hades and the maiden queen of hell , I will not neglect to greet first of all the gods beneath my roof .

sections.label: Section 610
sections.original: καὶ θῆρά γʼ ἐς φῶς τὸν τρίκρανον ἤγαγον .
sections.translation: Did you really go to the house of Hades , my son ?

sections.label: Section 611
sections.original: μάχῃ κρατήσας ἢ θεᾶς δωρήμασιν ;
sections.translation: Yes , and brought to the light that three-headed monster .

sections.label: Section 612
sections.original: μάχῃ · τὰ μυστῶν δʼ ὄργιʼ εὐτύχησʼ ἰδών .
sections.translation: Did you conquer him in fight , or receive him from the goddess ?

sections.label: Section 613
sections.original: ἦ καὶ κατʼ οἴκους ἐστὶν Εὐρυσθέως ὁ θήρ ;
sections.translation: In fight ; for I had been lucky enough to witness the rites of the initiated .

sections.label: Section 614
sections.original: Χθονίας νιν ἄλσος Ἑρμιών τʼ ἔχει πόλις .
sections.translation: Is the monster really lodged in the house of Eurystheus ?

sections.label: Section 615
sections.original: οὐδʼ οἶδεν Εὐρυσθεύς σε γῆς ἥκοντʼ ἄνω ;
sections.translation: The grove of Demeter and the city of Hermione have him now .

sections.label: Section 616
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδʼ · ἵνʼ ἐλθὼν τἀνθάδʼ εἰδείην πάρος .
sections.translation: Eurystheus does not know that you have returned to the upper world ?

sections.label: Section 617
sections.original: χρόνον δὲ πῶς τοσοῦτον ἦσθʼ ὑπὸ χθονί ;
sections.translation: He does not ; I came here first to learn your news .

sections.label: Section 618
sections.original: Θησέα κομίζων ἐχρόνισʼ ἐξ Ἅιδου , πάτερ .
sections.translation: How is it you were so long beneath the earth ?

sections.label: Section 619
sections.original: καὶ ποῦ ʼστιν ; ἢ γῆς πατρίδος οἴχεται πέδον ;
sections.translation: I stayed awhile attempting to bring back Theseus from Hades , father .

sections.label: Section 620
sections.original: βέβηκʼ Ἀθήνας νέρθεν ἄσμενος φυγών .
sections.translation: Where is he ? gone to his native land ?

sections.label: Section 621
sections.original:
ἀλλʼ εἶʼ , ὁμαρτεῖτʼ , ὦ τέκνʼ , ἐς δόμους πατρί ·
καλλίονές τἄρʼ εἴσοδοι τῶν ἐξόδων
πάρεισιν ὑμῖν . ἀλλὰ θάρσος ἴσχετε
καὶ νάματʼ ὄσσων μηκέτʼ ἐξανίετε ·
sections.translation: He set out for Athens , glad to have escaped from the lower world . Come now , children , attend your father to the house . My entering in is fairer in your eyes , I think , than my going out . Oh , take heart ,

sections.label: Section 625
sections.original:
σύ τʼ , ὦ γύναι μοι , σύλλογον ψυχῆς λαβὲ
τρόμου τε παῦσαι , καὶ μέθεσθʼ ἐμῶν πέπλων ·
οὐ γὰρ πτερωτὸς οὐδὲ φευξείω φίλους .
ἆ ,
οἵδʼ οὐκ ἀφιᾶσʼ , ἀλλʼ ἀνάπτονται πέπλων
τοσῷδε μᾶλλον · ὧδʼ ἔβητʼ ἐπὶ ξυροῦ ;
sections.translation: and no more let the tears stream from your eyes ; you too , my wife , collect your courage , cease from fear ; leave go my robe ; for I cannot fly away , nor have I any wish to flee from those I love . Ah ! they do not loose their hold , but cling to my garments

sections.label: Section 630
sections.original:
ἄξω λαβών γε τούσδʼ ἐφολκίδας χεροῖν ,
ναῦς δʼ ὣς ἐφέλξω · καὶ γὰρ οὐκ ἀναίνομαι
θεράπευμα τέκνων . πάντα τἀνθρώπων ἴσα ·
φιλοῦσι παῖδας οἵ τʼ ἀμείνονες βροτῶν
οἵ τʼ οὐδὲν ὄντες · χρήμασιν δὲ διάφοροι ·
sections.translation: all the more ; were you on the razor ’ s edge of danger ? Well , I must lead them , taking them by the hand to draw them after me , my little boats , like a ship when towing ; for I too do not reject the care of my children ; here all mankind are equal ; all love their children , both those of high estate

sections.label: Section 635
sections.original:
ἔχουσιν , οἳ δʼ οὔ · πᾶν δὲ φιλότεκνον γένος .
ἁ νεότας μοι φίλον αἰ-
εί · τὸ δὲ γῆρας ἄχθος
βαρύτερον Αἴτνας σκοπέλων
sections.translation: and those who are nothing ; it is wealth that makes distinctions among them ; some have , others want ; but all the human race loves its offspring .

sections.label: Section 1.637
sections.original:
ἐπὶ κρατὶ κεῖται , βλεφάρων
σκοτεινὸν φάος ἐπικαλύψαν .
sections.translation: Dear to me is youth always , but old age is hanging over my head , a burden heavier

sections.label: Section 1.640
sections.original:
μή μοι μήτʼ Ἀσιήτιδος
τυραννίδος ὄλβος εἴη ,
μὴ χρυσοῦ δώματα πλήρη
τᾶς ἥβας ἀντιλαβεῖν ,
sections.translation: than Aetna ’ s crags , casting its pall of gloom upon my eyes . Oh ! never may the wealth of Asia ’ s kings tempt me

sections.label: Section 1.645
sections.original:
ἃ καλλίστα μὲν ἐν ὄλβῳ ,
καλλίστα δʼ ἐν πενίᾳ .
τὸ δὲ λυγρὸν φόνιόν τε γῆ-
ρας μισῶ · κατὰ κυμάτων δʼ
sections.translation: to barter for houses stored with gold my happy youth , which is in wealth and poverty alike most fair ! But old age is gloomy and deadly ;

sections.label: Section 1.650
sections.original:
ἔρροι , μηδέ ποτʼ ὤφελεν
θνατῶν δώματα καὶ πόλεις
ἐλθεῖν , ἀλλὰ κατʼ αἰθέρʼ αἰ-
εὶ πτεροῖσι φορείσθω .
εἰ δὲ θεοῖς ἦν ξύνεσις
sections.translation: I hate it ; let it sink beneath the waves ! Would it had never found its way to the homes and towns of mortal men , but were still drifting on for ever down the wind .

sections.label: Section 1.655
sections.original:
καὶ σοφία κατʼ ἄνδρας ,
δίδυμον ἂν ἥβαν ἔφερον
φανερὸν χαρακτῆρʼ ἀρετᾶς
ὅσοισιν μέτα , κατθανόντες τʼ
sections.translation: Had the gods shown discernment and wisdom , as mortals count these things , men would have won youth twice over , a visible mark of worth

sections.label: Section 1.660
sections.original:
εἰς αὐγὰς πάλιν ἁλίου
δισσοὺς ἂν ἔβαν διαύλους ,
ἁ δυσγένεια δʼ ἁπλοῦν ἂν
εἶχεν ζόας βίοτον ,
καὶ τῷδʼ ἦν τούς τε κακοὺς ἂν
sections.translation: among whomever found , and after death these would have run a double course once more to the sun-light , while the low born would have had a single portion of life ;

sections.label: Section 1.665
sections.original:
γνῶναι καὶ τοὺς ἀγαθούς ,
ἴσον ἅτʼ ἐν νεφέλαισιν ἄ-
στρων ναύταις ἀριθμὸς πέλει .
νῦν δʼ οὐδεὶς ὅρος ἐκ θεῶν
χρηστοῖς οὐδὲ κακοῖς σαφής ,
sections.translation: and thus would it have been possible to distinguish the good and the bad , just as sailors know the number of the stars amid the clouds . But , as it is , the gods have set no certain boundary

sections.label: Section 1.670
sections.original:
ἀλλʼ εἱλισσόμενός τις αἰ-
ὼν πλοῦτον μόνον αὔξει .
οὐ παύσομαι τὰς Χάριτας
sections.translation: between good and bad , but time ’ s onward roll brings increase only to man ’ s wealth .

sections.label: Section 2.673
sections.original:
Μούσαις συγκαταμειγνύς ,
ἁδίσταν συζυγίαν .
sections.translation: Never will I cease to link in one the Graces and the Muses ,

sections.label: Section 2.675
sections.original:
μὴ ζῴην μετʼ ἀμουσίας ,
αἰεὶ δʼ ἐν στεφάνοισιν εἴ-
ην · ἔτι τοι γέρων ἀοι-
sections.translation: sweetest union . Never may I live among uneducated boors , but ever may I find a place among the crowned !

sections.label: Section 2.680
sections.original:
δὸς κελαδεῖ Μναμοσύναν ·
ἔτι τὰν Ἡρακλέους
καλλίνικον ἀείδω
παρά τε Βρόμιον οἰνοδόταν
παρά τε χέλυος ἑπτατόνου
μολπὰν καὶ Λίβυν αὐλόν ·
οὔπω καταπαύσομεν
sections.translation: Yes , still the aged singer lifts up his voice of bygone memories : still is my song of the triumphs of Heracles , whether Bromius the giver of wine is near , or the strains of the seven-stringed lyre and the Libyan pipe are rising ;

sections.label: Section 2.685
sections.original:
Μούσας , αἵ μʼ ἐχόρευσαν .
παιᾶνα μὲν Δηλιάδες
sections.translation: not yet will I cease to sing the Muses ’ praise , my patrons in the dance .

sections.label: Section 2.687
sections.original:
ὑμνοῦσʼ ἀμφὶ πύλας τὸν
Λατοῦς εὔπαιδα γόνον
εἱλίσσουσαι καλλίχορον ·
sections.translation: The maids of Delos raise their song of joy , circling round the temple gates in honor of Leto ’ s fair son ,

sections.label: Section 2.690
sections.original:
παιᾶνας δʼ ἐπὶ σοῖς μελά-
θροις κύκνος ὣς γέρων ἀοι-
δὸς πολιᾶν ἐκ γενύων
κελαδήσω · τὸ γὰρ εὖ
sections.translation: the graceful dancer ; so I with my old lips will cry aloud songs of joy at your palace-doors , like the swan , aged singer ; for there is a good

sections.label: Section 2.695
sections.original:
τοῖς ὕμνοισιν ὑπάρχει ·
Διὸς ὁ παῖς · τᾶς δʼ εὐγενίας
πλέον ὑπερβάλλων ἀρετᾷ
μοχθήσας τὸν ἄκυμον
θῆκεν βίοτον βροτοῖς
sections.translation: theme for minstrelsy ; he is the son of Zeus ; yet high above his noble birth tower his deeds of prowess , for his toil secured this life of calm for man ,

sections.label: Section 2.700
sections.original: πέρσας δείματα θηρῶν .
sections.translation: having destroyed all fearsome beasts .

sections.label: Section 701
sections.original:
ἐς καιρὸν οἴκων Ἀμφιτρύων ἔξω περᾷ ·
χρόνος γὰρ ἤδη δαρὸς ἐξ ὅτου πέπλοις
κοσμεῖσθε σῶμα καὶ νεκρῶν ἀγάλμασιν .
ἀλλʼ εἶα , παῖδας καὶ δάμαρθʼ Ἡρακλέους
sections.translation: Amphitryon , it is high time you came forth from the palace ; you have been too long arraying yourselves in the robes and trappings of the dead . Come , bid the wife and children of Heracles

sections.label: Section 705
sections.original:
ἔξω κέλευε τῶνδε φαίνεσθαι δόμων ,
ἐφʼ οἷς ὑπέστητʼ αὐτεπάγγελτοι θανεῖν .
sections.translation: show themselves outside the house , to die on the conditions you yourselves offered .

sections.label: Section 707
sections.original:
ἄναξ , διώκεις μʼ ἀθλίως πεπραγότα
ὕβριν θʼ ὑβρίζεις ἐπὶ θανοῦσι τοῖς ἐμοῖς ·
ἃ χρῆν σε μετρίως , κεἰ κρατεῖς , σπουδὴν ἔχειν .
sections.translation: Lord , you persecute me in my misery and heap insult upon me over and above the loss of my son ; you should have been more moderate in your zeal , though you are my lord and master .

sections.label: Section 710
sections.original:
ἐπεὶ δʼ ἀνάγκην προστίθης ἡμῖν θανεῖν ,
στέργειν ἀνάγκη · δραστέον δʼ ἃ σοὶ δοκεῖ .
sections.translation: But since you impose death ’ s necessity on me , I must acquiesce ; what you wish must be done .

sections.label: Section 712
sections.original: ποῦ δῆτα Μεγάρα ; ποῦ τέκνʼ Ἀλκμήνης γόνου ;
sections.translation: Now , where is Megara ? where are the children of Alcmena ’ s son ?

sections.label: Section 713
sections.original: δοκῶ μὲν αὐτήν , ὡς θύραθεν εἰκάσαι —
sections.translation: She , I believe , so far as I can guess from outside —

sections.label: Section 714
sections.original: τί χρῆμα δόξης ; τοῦ δʼ ἔχεις τεκμήριον ;
sections.translation: What grounds do you have to base your fancy on ?

sections.label: Section 715
sections.original: ἱκέτιν πρὸς ἁγνοῖς Ἑστίας θάσσειν βάθροις ⋯
sections.translation: Is sitting as a suppliant on the altar ’ s hallowed steps —

sections.label: Section 716
sections.original: ἀνόνητά γʼ ἱκετεύουσαν ἐκσῷσαι βίον .
sections.translation: Imploring them quite uselessly to save her life .

sections.label: Section 717
sections.original: καὶ τὸν θανόντα γʼ ἀνακαλεῖν μάτην πόσιν .
sections.translation: And calling on her dead husband , in vain .

sections.label: Section 718
sections.original: ὃ δʼ οὐ πάρεστιν οὐδὲ μὴ μόλῃ ποτέ .
sections.translation: He is nowhere near , and he certainly will never come .

sections.label: Section 719
sections.original: οὔκ , εἴ γε μή τις θεῶν ἀναστήσειέ νιν .
sections.translation: No , unless perhaps a god should raise him from the dead .

sections.label: Section 720
sections.original: χώρει πρὸς αὐτὴν κἀκκόμιζε δωμάτων .
sections.translation: Go to her and bring her from the palace .

sections.label: Section 721
sections.original: μέτοχος ἂν εἴην τοῦ φόνου δράσας τόδε .
sections.translation: By doing so I should become an accomplice in her murder .

sections.label: Section 722
sections.original:
ἡμεῖς , ἐπειδὴ σοὶ τόδʼ ἔστʼ ἐνθύμιον ,
οἱ δειμάτων ἔξωθεν ἐκπορεύσομεν
σὺν μητρὶ παῖδας . δεῦρʼ ἕπεσθε , πρόσπολοι ,
sections.translation: Since you have this scruple , I , who have left fear behind , will myself bring out the mother and her children . Follow me , servants ,

sections.label: Section 725
sections.original: ὡς ἂν σχολὴν λύσωμεν ἄσμενοι πόνων .
sections.translation: that we may joyfully put an end to this delay of our work .

sections.label: Section 726
sections.original:
σὺ δʼ οὖν ἴθʼ , ἔρχῃ δʼ οἷ χρεών · τὰ δʼ ἄλλʼ ἴσως
ἄλλῳ μελήσει . προσδόκα δὲ δρῶν κακῶς
κακόν τι πράξειν . ὦ γέροντες , ἐς καλὸν
στείχει , βρόχοισι δʼ ἀρκύων γενήσεται
sections.translation: Then go your way along the path of fate ; for what remains , maybe another will provide . Expect for your evil deeds to find some trouble yourself . Ah ! my aged friends , he is marching fairly to his doom ; soon will he lie entangled in the snare

sections.label: Section 730
sections.original:
ξιφηφόροισι , τοὺς πέλας δοκῶν κτενεῖν
ὁ παγκάκιστος . εἶμι δʼ , ὡς ἴδω νεκρὸν
πίπτοντʼ · ἔχει γὰρ ἡδονὰς θνῄσκων ἀνὴρ
ἐχθρὸς τίνων τε τῶν δεδραμένων δίκην .
sections.translation: of the sword , thinking to slay his neighbors , the villain ! I will go , to see him fall dead ; for the sight of a foe being slain and paying the penalty of his misdeeds affords pleasurable feelings .

sections.label: Section 1.735
sections.original:
— μεταβολὰ κακῶν · μέγας ὁ πρόσθʼ ἄναξ
πάλιν ὑποστρέφει βίοτον ἐξ Ἅιδα .
ἰώ ·
δίκα καὶ θεῶν παλίρρους πότμος .
sections.translation: Evil has changed sides ; he who was once a mighty king is now turning his life backward into the road to Hades . Hail to you ! Justice and heavenly retribution .
